text:
Market today: Oil sliding on Iran-US talks eases energy cost pressure, which is a mild positive for tech margins. Meta cloud ambitions are moving markets - helping mega-cap tech sentiment but creating collateral damage in AI infrastructure names like CoreWeave. Broad tape feels constructive but not explosive.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.94%) is the one demanding respect - leveraged 3X decay is real, it's on margin, and the stop at $200 is close to current price ($216). One bad semiconductor session and you're stopped. SPAL (-13.96%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO with a razor-thin stop at $21.38 vs. current $21.64 - that stop could get hit on a sneeze. Margin amplifies both of these hard. SMH (-2.5%) is dragging too - chips broadly under pressure.

Watch: Whether SMH stabilizes or breaks lower - it signals where SOXL goes next. Also watch if Meta cloud news creates more selling in chip/AI infrastructure names that bleed into SMH.

Bottom line: Your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) are your live grenades right now - know your exit before the market decides for you.
plays: SOXL ($216.49, -14.94%): Stop at $200 is your line. Consider trimming now given leveraged decay + margin exposure - don't wait for the stop to save you.
SPAL ($21.64): Stop is $21.38 - only 26 cents away. This can gap through on any chip weakness. Size check: is this position small enough to absorb a full stop-out on margin?

CRWD ($772.74, +7.4%): Only name in the green with real momentum. Scanner shows entry zone $769-770, stop $763.85. If it dips into that zone, it's a clean add - but only if SOXL/SPAL aren't already stressing your margin.
